description: Primary-care/common chronic medication enrichment batch. Public label/guideline facts only; intended for human clinical review before promotion.

substances.name: Tirzepatide

routes.clinicalRelevance: No CYP-mediated clearance; initiation and dose escalation can delay gastric emptying and affect absorption timing of oral medicines.

substances.transporterRelevance: No clinically dominant transporter pathway identified in label-level public facts.

substances.name: Eplerenone

routes.clinicalRelevance: Primary metabolic route; strong CYP3A inhibitors are clinically important.

substances.transporterRelevance: Transporter effects are not the main clinically actionable interaction mechanism.

substances.name: Sacubitril/Valsartan

routes.clinicalRelevance: Sacubitril is activated to neprilysin inhibitor LBQ657.
Renal function and potassium context shape safety.

substances.transporterRelevance: OATP transporter effects are label-relevant for some statins but less central than ACE-inhibitor and potassium workflows.

substances.name: Insulin Glargine

routes.clinicalRelevance: No CYP metabolism; interaction relevance is pharmacodynamic hypoglycemia and masking of warning symptoms.

substances.transporterRelevance: No clinically dominant transporter interaction pathway.

substances.name: Chlorthalidone

routes.clinicalRelevance: Renal handling and electrolyte effects drive clinically meaningful interactions.

substances.transporterRelevance: Renal tubular electrolyte handling is clinically relevant; no specific CYP transporter substrate role is the main signal.

substances.name: Torsemide

routes.clinicalRelevance: Volume and electrolyte changes drive lithium and digoxin risk.

substances.transporterRelevance: Transporter interactions are less actionable than renal clearance and electrolyte context.

substances.name: Donepezil

routes.clinicalRelevance: Metabolic route with inhibitor exposure relevance.
Metabolic route with inhibitor exposure relevance.

substances.transporterRelevance: No primary transporter interaction signal in public label facts.

substances.name: Memantine

routes.clinicalRelevance: Urine alkalinization can reduce elimination and increase exposure.

substances.transporterRelevance: Renal pH-dependent clearance is clinically more actionable than CYP/transporter modulation.

ddiPairs.mechanism: Improved glycemic control can increase hypoglycemia risk when tirzepatide is added to insulin.
ddiPairs.effect: Consider insulin dose reduction and closer glucose monitoring during initiation and escalation.

ddiPairs.mechanism: Itraconazole strongly inhibits CYP3A4, the main eplerenone clearance route.
ddiPairs.effect: Contraindicated due to high eplerenone exposure and hyperkalemia risk.

ddiPairs.mechanism: Neprilysin inhibition plus ACE inhibition increases bradykinin-mediated angioedema risk.
ddiPairs.effect: Contraindicated; use a 36-hour washout when switching to or from an ACE inhibitor.

ddiPairs.mechanism: Thiazide-like diuretics can reduce lithium renal clearance.
ddiPairs.effect: High lithium toxicity risk; avoid if possible or reduce dose and monitor serum lithium.

ddiPairs.mechanism: Loop-diuretic hypokalemia and hypomagnesemia can increase digoxin arrhythmia risk.
ddiPairs.effect: Monitor potassium, magnesium, renal function, and digoxin toxicity signs.

ddiPairs.mechanism: Donepezil vagotonic effects plus beta-blockade can increase bradycardia or heart-block risk.
ddiPairs.effect: Monitor pulse, syncope, and conduction symptoms after initiation or dose changes.

ddiPairs.mechanism: Urine alkalinization can reduce memantine renal elimination.
ddiPairs.effect: Avoid sustained alkalinization or monitor for dizziness, confusion, and CNS adverse effects.
